design-system-quick-start--docs.name: Docs
design-system-quick-start--docs.title: Design System/Quick Start
design-system-quick-start--docs.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import { DTabs, DLayout, DBox } from '../src';
# Dynamic Framework {import.meta.env.STORYBOOK_DEV === 'true' ? ( ) : ( )} Dynamic Framework components come with production-ready code, design guides, and an active community of financial experience experts. Accessing the [main site](https://dynamicframework.dev/) provides additional information and resources. The [Github Repository](https://github.com/dynamic-framework/dynamic-ui) is available for review and collaboration.
### Requirements - node@22+
### Install ```shell npm install @dynamic-framework/ui-react@latest ``` ```shell yarn add @dynamic-framework/ui-react@latest ``` ```shell pnpm add @dynamic-framework/ui-react@latest ```
### Getting started with templates Modyo provides a base of templates with the framework configured ready to use. The simplest is ```dynamic-react-base-template```, it is recommended to download this template using the [CLI](https://docs.modyo.com/es/platform/channels/cli.html). For further exploration and contribution, you can visit the [GitHub repository.](https://github.com/dynamic-framework/dynamic-react-base-template) ```shell npx @modyo/cli@latest get dynamic-react-base-template ```
### Foundations Dynamic Framework is based on Bootstrap 5, with a standardization layer that unifies color tokens, typography, spacing, and utility variants (hover:, dark:) to deliver consistent, accessible interfaces. We extend Bootstrap with additional utilities and responsive variants to accelerate UI building. Examples include expanded spacing (0–30), extended grids (g-col-13…24), typography utilities (fs-body, display-*), overlay opacity, text truncation, text-wrap, vertical align, responsive width/height, and more.
#### Typography - Default font family: Jost, optimized for UI legibility - Hierarchy: Headings (h1–h6), Display (`.display-1` … `.display-6`), Font-size utilities (`.fs-1` … `.fs-6`, `.fs-body`) See: Design System/Foundations/Typography.
#### Colors (Semantic states) Use semantic color tokens for clarity and accessibility: - Primary, Success, Warning, Danger, Info, Secondary, Light, Dark - Text: `.text-{token}`, Background tints: `.bg-{token}-{25|50|100|200|300|400|500|600|700|800|900}`, Border: `.border-{token}` - Light variants are intended for use on dark backgrounds to ensure contrast.
Primary

Action or highlight

Success

Positive state

Warning

Cautionary state

Danger

Error/Destructive

Info

Informational

Secondary

Neutral/Complementary

Dark

Dark surfaces

```html
Warning
Danger
```

Light variants (e.g., .text-light), are designed for dark backgrounds.


#### Spacing system Spacing utilities extended to 0–30 with responsive variants. - Margin: `.m{t|b|s|e|x|y}-{0..30}`; Padding: `.p{t|b|s|e|x|y}-{0..30}`
.p-2
.mt-15
.px-30 .py-2
```html
...
```
#### Grid & layout Leverage Bootstrap grid: Container (.container), Rows (.row), Columns (.col, .col-6, .col-md-4 …). You can use the classic <div class="row"> approach, or our CSS Grid-based column system via <DLayout />. See: Design System/Components/Layout.
.col-6
.col-6
.col-md-4
.col-md-8
```html
...
``` DLayout (CSS Grid) example: ```tsx import { DLayout, DBox } from '@dynamic-framework/ui-react'; Sidebar Main Content ```
Sidebar Main Content

#### Dark mode `dark:` utilities layer on Bootstrap and also support automatic `prefers-color-scheme`. See: Design System/Examples/Dark Utilities.
Auto/system dark tint
Forced dark via .dark
```html
...
...
```
### Accessibility guidance - Use semantic HTML (headings, lists, buttons, links) for structure - Ensure WCAG AA contrast for text/background (e.g., danger on light bg) - Preserve focus states and keyboard navigation (`:focus-visible`) - Provide clear feedback: success, warning, danger, info colors - Prefer motion small/medium durations (150–250ms) for UI transitions
### Notes Dynamic Framework is a Bootstrap overlay: typography (Jost), expanded palettes, utility variants (`hover:`, `dark:`), and consistent tokens for UI components.

design-system-examples-icons--docs.name: Docs
design-system-examples-icons--docs.title: Design System/Examples/Icons
design-system-examples-icons--docs.content: import { Meta, Unstyled, Source, } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; ## Icons The `DIcon` component is a versatile icon component that can be configured to use different icon fonts. It supports both traditional icon fonts and material-style icon fonts. ### Configuration The `DIcon` component is configured using the `DContextProvider`. The `DContextProvider` accepts an `icon` object with the following properties: - `familyPrefix`: The prefix for the icon class (e.g., `bi-` for Bootstrap). - `familyClass`: The class for the icon family (e.g., `bi-icon` for Bootstrap icons). - `materialStyle`: A boolean flag that determines how the icon is rendered. If `true`, the icon is rendered with the name of the icon in the content of the tag. If `false`, the icon is rendered with the class of the icon font. `} language="html" /> ### Material Style Flag This flag comes in handy when you have icon fonts that need to be included as the content of the tag. Following example show the output of `DIcon` component with and without the `materialStyle` flag. `} language="html" /> user `} language="html" /> ## Example Usage #### Font Awesome Icons To use Font Awesome icons, configure the `DContextProvider` as follows: ```jsx ``` #### Material Icons To use Material icons, configure the `DContextProvider` as follows: ```jsx ```

design-system-foundations-color-tokens--.name: Docs
design-system-foundations-color-tokens--.title: Design System/Foundations/Color Tokens
design-system-foundations-color-tokens--.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
# Color Tokens Dynamic Framework provides semantic color tokens with extended shades (25, 50, 100 … 900) for text, backgrounds, borders, badges, and buttons. Tokens: Primary, Success, Warning, Danger, Info, Secondary, Light, Dark. Light variants are designed for dark backgrounds.
### Text colors Use text tokens to communicate state and semantics.

.text-primary

.text-success

.text-warning

.text-danger

.text-info

.text-secondary

.text-dark

Light variant (for dark backgrounds)

.text-light (use on dark bg)
```html

Success text

Error text

```
### Background tints (shades 25–900) Apply subtle state backgrounds with shade suffixes.
.bg-primary-25
.bg-primary-100
.bg-primary-900
.bg-success-100
.bg-warning-100
.bg-danger-100
.bg-info-100
.bg-secondary-100
```html
.bg-primary-25
.bg-primary-100
.bg-primary-900
```
### Borders Use border tokens to frame elements.
.border-primary
.border-success
.border-warning
.border-danger
.border-info
.border-secondary
```html
…
```
### Badges Badges support semantic color tokens via text-bg-*.
Primary Success Warning Danger Info Secondary Dark

Light variant (for dark backgrounds)

Light
```html Success ```
### Buttons Buttons support solid and outline variants with tokens.

Light variant (for dark backgrounds)

```html ```
### Alerts Semantic alerts styled with color tokens.
Primary alert — informative message.
Success alert — action completed.
Warning alert — proceed carefully.
Danger alert — something went wrong.
Info alert — additional context.
Secondary alert — neutral notice.
Dark alert — emphasis on dark tones.
```html
Success alert
```
### Overlays and opacity Use overlay opacity with state tints.
Overlay 50%
```html
…
```
### Dark mode and utilities - Prefer .text-light over dark surfaces. - Combine tokens with dark: for system/forced dark contexts. See: Design System/Examples/Dark Utilities and Foundations/Extended Utilities.
## Accessibility guidance - Ensure WCAG AA contrast when pairing text and backgrounds. - Use semantic tokens consistently for clear communication.

design-system-foundations-css-distributi.name: Docs
design-system-foundations-css-distributi.title: Design System/Foundations/CSS Distribution
design-system-foundations-css-distributi.content:
import { Meta, Unstyled } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import { DAlert } from '../../src/components';
# CSS Distribution & Customization Starting from version 2.3.0, only `dynamic-ui.css` and `dynamic-ui.min.css` are distributed. The previous variants `dynamic-ui-root.css` and `dynamic-ui-non-root.css` have been removed to simplify upgrades and avoid desynchronization issues. ### How to Customize 1. **Include the main CSS:** ```html ``` 2. **Override variables:** Add your own stylesheet or snippet after the main CSS to override any design tokens or variables: ```css :root { --bs-primary: #ff0055; --bs-body-font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; /* ...other overrides... */ } ``` 3. **Safe upgrades:** All default variables are included in the main bundle, so new tokens are available automatically and removed ones do not break the theme.
### Example: Custom Theme Snippet ```html ```
### Migration Guide - If you previously used `dynamic-ui-root.css` or `dynamic-ui-non-root.css`, switch to `dynamic-ui.css` and apply your overrides as shown above. - For advanced theming, refer to the [Design Tokens](?path=/docs/design-system-foundations-design-tokens--docs) page and [docs.modyo.com](https://docs.modyo.com/en/dynamic/).
### FAQ **Q: What happens if there are two `:root` blocks?**
A: The last one loaded wins for each variable, thanks to CSS cascade. You can safely override any token after loading the main CSS. **Q: How do I keep my theme up to date?**
A: Since all tokens are included in the main bundle, you only need to override what you want. New tokens are available automatically after upgrades. **Q: Where can I find a list of available tokens?**
A: See the [Design Tokens](?path=/docs/design-system-foundations-design-tokens--docs) page or Storybook for a visual reference.

design-system-foundations-extended-utili.name: Docs
design-system-foundations-extended-utili.title: Design System/Foundations/Extended Utilities
design-system-foundations-extended-utili.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
# Utilities Overview Dynamic Framework extends Bootstrap utilities for speed and flexibility. Bootstrap’s core utilities are not all responsive by default; we provide responsive variants and extensions across display, alignment, spacing, typography, and more. Below are notable additions from our utilities layer.
### Variants: hover: and dark: - Hover Utilities: Design System/Examples/Hover Utilities - Dark Utilities: Design System/Examples/Dark Utilities ```html Danger on hover
Success in dark
```
### Spacing (0–30) - Margin: `.m{t|b|s|e|x|y}-{0..30}`; Padding: `.p{t|b|s|e|x|y}-{0..30}` (responsive) ```html
...
```
### Extended display and alignment - Display: `.d-{inline|inline-block|block|table|table-row|table-cell|flex|inline-flex|none|grid|table-header-group}` (responsive) - Vertical align: `.v-align-{baseline|top|bottom|text-top|text-bottom|sub|super|middle}` (responsive) ```html
...
```
### Grid columns (CSS Grid) - `.g-col-{13…24}` for larger grid spans (responsive) ```html
6 cols
18 cols
```
### Typography utilities - Display font sizes: `.display-{1…6}` map to `--bs-fs-display-*` via `--bs-display-font-size` - Body font size: `.fs-body` - Line-height: `.lh-1`, `.lh-sm`, `.lh-base`, `.lh-lg` - Text wrap: `.text-wrap-{balance|normal|truncate|wrap}` - Text truncate (lines): `.text-truncate-{1…6}` (responsive) ```html
Display 4

Balanced wrapping

Long text…
``` See: Design System/Foundations/Typography.
### Color utilities - Text/background/border colors include palettes and grays: `.text-{token}`, `.bg-{token}-{25|50|100|200|300|400|500|600|700|800|900}`, `.border-{token}` - Overlay opacity: `.overlay-{0|25|50|75|100}` (uses CSS var) - Opacity and text-opacity: support `40` in addition to Bootstrap defaults ```html
Overlayed block
```
### Size utilities - Width/Height: `.w-*`, `.h-*` extended to be responsive - Font-weight and position: responsive variants enabled ```html
...
```
### Cursor utilities - `.cursor-{auto|default|pointer|not-allowed|wait|text|crosshair|zoom-in|zoom-out|grab|grabbing}` ```html ```
### Animation utilities - `.fade-in` — applies a quick opacity transition from `0` to `1` using `ease-in-out` (duration: `0.3s`). ```html
This content fades in on render
``` Useful for conditionally rendered elements, modals, alerts, or any content that appears dynamically. The animation runs once on mount and does not loop.
### Layout component (DLayout) For more advanced responsive grid layouts, the `DLayout` component provides a declarative API on top of these CSS utilities, with customizable `columns` and responsive `gap*` props. Use `DLayout.Pane` with responsive `cols*` props to control individual pane widths, alongside standard `className`, `style`, and `dataAttributes` support. See: Design System/Components/Layout.

design-system-foundations-typography--do.name: Docs
design-system-foundations-typography--do.title: Design System/Foundations/Typography
design-system-foundations-typography--do.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
# Typography Foundations Dynamic Framework standardizes typography on top of Bootstrap 5 using the Jost typeface, a clear size scale, semantic text colors, and helpful utilities for weight, line-height, wrapping, and truncation.
### Type scale and display styles - Headings: h1–h6 and heading utility classes `.h1` … `.h6` - Display styles: `.display-1` … `.display-6` - Size utilities: `.fs-1` … `.fs-6`, `.small` - Body size: `.fs-body`
Display 1
Display 4

Heading H1

Heading H3

Heading utility (.h1)

Heading utility (.h4)

Text with .fs-4

Body default (.fs-body)

Small text (.small)

```css /* Display utilities map to CSS vars used by type rendering */ .display-1 { --bs-display-font-size: var(--bs-fs-display-1); } .display-2 { --bs-display-font-size: var(--bs-fs-display-2); } .display-3 { --bs-display-font-size: var(--bs-fs-display-3); } .display-4 { --bs-display-font-size: var(--bs-fs-display-4); } .display-5 { --bs-display-font-size: var(--bs-fs-display-5); } .display-6 { --bs-display-font-size: var(--bs-fs-display-6); } /* Base type consumes it: font-size: var(--bs-display-font-size) */ /* Size utilities */ .fs-1 { font-size: var(--bs-fs-1); } .fs-2 { font-size: var(--bs-fs-2); } .fs-3 { font-size: var(--bs-fs-3); } .fs-4 { font-size: var(--bs-fs-4); } .fs-5 { font-size: var(--bs-fs-5); } .fs-6 { font-size: var(--bs-fs-6); } .small { font-size: var(--bs-fs-small); } /* Body size utility */ .fs-body { font-size: var(--bs-body-font-size); } ```
### Semantic text colors Use `.text-{token}` with semantic states and extended palettes: - Tokens: Primary, Success, Warning, Danger, Info, Secondary, Light, Dark - Shades: `{25|50|100|200|300|400|500|600|700|800|900}` for background tints: `.bg-{token}-{shade}` - Light variants are intended for dark backgrounds

Primary text

Success text

Warning text

Danger text

Info text

Secondary text

Dark text

Text-light over dark background

### Font weight and line-height - Font weight utilities (responsive enabled): `.fw-light`, `.fw-normal`, `.fw-semibold`, `.fw-bold` - Line-height utilities: `.lh-1`, `.lh-sm`, `.lh-base`, `.lh-lg`

Light weight

Normal weight

Semibold weight

Bold weight

Line-height 1

Line-height sm

Line-height base

Line-height lg

```css /* Line-height utilities map to variables */ .lh-sm { line-height: var(--bs-lh-sm); } .lh-base { line-height: var(--bs-lh-base); } .lh-lg { line-height: var(--bs-lh-lg); } ```
### Text decoration and font style - Underline: `.text-decoration-underline` - Line-through: `.text-decoration-line-through` - Italic: `.fst-italic`

Underlined text

Line-through text

Italic text


### Text wrapping and truncation - Balanced wrapping: `.text-wrap-balance` - Normal wrapping: `.text-wrap-normal`; Force wrap: `.text-wrap-wrap` - Truncation by lines (responsive): `.text-truncate-{1..6}`

Balanced wrapping strives for visually even lines for better readability across varying widths.

Force wrapping when you need long identifiers or URLs to break across lines gracefully.

Long text that will be truncated to three lines. Long text that will be truncated to three lines. Long text that will be truncated to three lines. Long text that will be truncated to three lines with additional content to ensure overflow beyond three lines in typical widths. This sentence adds even more content so the ellipsis appears reliably across viewports. Yet another clause to extend the content further.

```css /* Text wrap utility values */ .text-wrap-balance { text-wrap: balance; } .text-wrap-normal { text-wrap: normal; } .text-wrap-truncate{ text-wrap: truncate; } .text-wrap-wrap { text-wrap: wrap; } /* Truncate uses CSS vars and -webkit-line-clamp under the hood */ ```
### Dark mode and hover variants for text - Dark mode variant: `.dark:text-{token}` adapts text color in dark contexts - Hover variant: `.hover:text-{token}` provides interactive feedback on text ```html Hover to see danger color
Text turns success in dark
```
